place: Glens Falls New York, New York

facts.text: Glens Falls got its name in 1788 when Colonel Johannes Glen, who built mills there, replaced the earlier Quaker name Wing’s Falls.
The Crandall Public Library, founded in 1893, moved to its current building in 1931 and remains a historic community hub.
Founded in 1897 by twelve local physicians, Glens Falls Hospital was created to serve the entire Upper Hudson River Valley.
The Chapman Historical Museum, housed in the Zopher Delong House, showcases local history through rotating exhibits and seasonal displays.
Adirondack Community College opened its doors in Glens Falls in 1961, becoming a cornerstone of regional higher education.
The Glens Falls Symphony began as a community orchestra in 1977 at Adirondack Community College before evolving into the Adirondack Philharmonia.
